decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and validates a wastewater framework combining a two-tiered early-warning system with trend forecasting for norovirus.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and validation of an early-warning framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Directly identifies false alarms and missed warnings from traditional case-average systems under climate non-stationarity and proposes a hybrid alternative.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The perspective advocates operational meteorological triggers and probabilistic models for dengue early warning.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Documents the establishment and operation of a nationwide wastewater surveillance system that generated infectious-disease early warnings.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Reports a concrete national implementation and its observed efficacy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates laboratory and field performance of GeneXpert-based environmental monitoring as an avian-influenza early-warning approach.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides controlled and field feasibility evidence for the proposed warning approach.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews RSV evolution and recommends stronger genomic monitoring, but does not develop, apply, critique, validate, correct, or implement a specific early-warning method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly recommends genomic monitoring and multisource integration to improve warning of abnormal RSV activity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and evaluates an RF-based warning system using a historical-case threshold, reporting sensitivity, specificity, and AUC.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and validation of a forecasting-based early-warning system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The scoping review directly identifies validation, dataset-dependence, heterogeneity, and outcome-reporting limitations in AI/ML approaches to AMR-clone early warning.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Method-focused review mapping approaches, gaps, and opportunities for early warning.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Measures WNV seroprevalence retrospectively and only concludes that future donor monitoring could serve as an early-warning system; it does not actually apply or evaluate such a system.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Potential utility is proposed, but no concrete early-warning method is used or assessed.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates an EWARS Mobile pilot using surveillance-system assessment frameworks and reports completeness, timeliness, usefulness, acceptability, flexibility, and simplicity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of an operational early-warning alert and response system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops hybrid DWT-SARMA-LSTM forecasting and dynamic alarm/epidemic thresholds as a more responsive dengue warning basis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and comparison against traditional thresholding.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Systematically assesses 36 social-media platforms for relevance, extraction mechanisms, and policy compliance for automated public-health early warning.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides explicit suitability criteria and comparative assessment for an automated warning system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Directly argues that Nipah early warning is misframed as reactive outbreak detection and identifies structural causes of delayed signal recognition.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Outlines an anticipatory framework linking risk assessment, One Health integration, thresholds, signals, and preparedness actions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates basic and effective reproduction numbers from epidemic curves and attack rates for both waves of the 1968 influenza pandemic.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete reproduction-number analysis of historical influenza-pandemic data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates and compares seasonal-influenza reproduction numbers across Brazilian states using mortality time series.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete empirical reproduction-number analysis of influenza surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates pathogen-specific basic reproduction numbers from laboratory-confirmed hand, foot, and mouth disease outbreak curves.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to EV71 and Coxsackievirus A16 outbreaks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses several epidemic-curve and spatial approaches to estimate farm-, phase-, and epidemic-level reproduction numbers for infectious salmon anemia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete reproduction-number analysis of a Chilean animal outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a mathematical model of outbreak sizes, detection, and hospital stay to estimate and compare single-admission reproduction numbers for MRSA genotypes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hospital-transmission application to livestock-associated MRSA.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares six estimation methods and shows that HIV basic-reproduction-number estimates depend strongly on the method and representation of sexual-activity variation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of method-specific differences and sources of underestimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates robustness of dengue basic-reproduction-number estimates across deterministic and stochastic models and multiple estimation methodologies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit cross-model and cross-method comparison of reproduction-number estimates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Infers the effective reproduction number and outbreak probability from US measles introductions and transmission-chain sizes.
Estimates reproduction numbers from assembled human H5N1 and H7N9 case and cluster data to compare transmissibility and pandemic risk.
Presents a Bayesian melding approach for estimating tuberculosis basic reproduction numbers from a deterministic model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological proposal demonstrated across Indian states and union territories.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates the initial reproduction number and mosquito density from data for Japan's first autochthonous dengue outbreak in 70 years.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete empirical analysis of a dengue outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates enterovirus-specific basic reproduction numbers from national laboratory-linked hand, foot, and mouth disease outbreak records.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Singapore HFMD surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes a Bayesian modeling framework for estimating serogroup-specific basic reproduction numbers from cross-sectional prevalence data and reconstructing infection dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a generalizable estimation framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates total and sexual-transmission reproduction numbers for the 2015 Zika outbreak using exponential case growth and a vector-plus-direct-transmission model.
Estimates Zika basic reproduction numbers from surveillance notifications and compares them with dengue-outbreak estimates in Rio de Janeiro.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete comparative analysis of Zika and dengue transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates dengue reproduction numbers from post-monsoon outbreak growth rates and biological parameters, with Monte Carlo uncertainty analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete reproduction-number analysis of Ahmedabad dengue incidence.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits an SIR model to verified 2015 South Korean MERS outbreak data to estimate the basic reproduction number.
Fits an incidence-decay model to MERS line lists to estimate reproduction numbers for nosocomial outbreaks in Saudi Arabia and South Korea.
Uses preliminary epidemiological data and a mosquito-transmission model to estimate the basic reproduction number for an Italian chikungunya outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete analysis of the 2017 Central Italy outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Devises a renewal-process model and Latin-hypercube uncertainty analysis to estimate diphtheria reproduction numbers when baseline immunity is poorly known.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for uncertain refugee-camp data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a vector-borne epidemic model and derives three parameter-estimation methods for reproduction-number analysis of cutaneous leishmaniasis outbreaks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model and estimation-method development with two outbreak demonstrations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses four established estimation methods on US influenza data to calculate reproduction numbers and corresponding vaccination coverage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete national and state-level influenza analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates early COVID-19 reproduction numbers while explicitly varying reporting rates and demonstrates substantial reporting-rate sensitivity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Evaluates a specific source of bias in early reproduction-number estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an SEIR model and reported or externally predicted case totals to estimate the basic reproduction number of 2019-nCoV.
Compares exponential-growth, maximum-likelihood, sequential-Bayesian, and time-dependent methods and identifies the best-fitting approach for early Wuhan data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit comparison of four reproduction-number estimation methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates SARS-CoV-2 reproduction numbers in Iran using generalized growth and epidemic-doubling-time methods before and after distancing interventions.
Fits a stochastic transmission model to early Korean and Italian COVID-19 data to estimate basic reproduction numbers under alternative growth-start assumptions.
Estimates COVID-19 reproduction numbers in four countries and performs sensitivity analyses showing dependence on assumed generation time.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit assessment of estimation sensitivity to generation-time assumptions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an exponential-growth model and Poisson likelihood to estimate basic and time-varying reproduction numbers for early COVID-19 spread in Africa.
Uses transmission pairs to estimate serial intervals, presymptomatic transmission, and time-varying reproduction numbers during Vietnam's early COVID-19 response.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete evaluation of SARS-CoV-2 transmission under national control measures.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates daily reproduction numbers from South Korean COVID-19 data and relates their decline to aggressive diagnostic testing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete early-pandemic transmission and intervention analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses laboratory-confirmed Omani case data and R software libraries to estimate time-varying reproduction numbers by nationality and monitor intervention effects.
Uses reported Indian case incidence, exponential growth, and time-dependent methods to estimate national and state reproduction numbers over time.
Estimates the effective reproduction number of Chile's 2018–2019 imported-measles outbreak and derives implied population immunity.
Calculates metropolitan time-varying reproduction numbers and relates their trajectories to mitigation measures and temperature.
Estimates time-varying reproduction numbers before and during lockdown to assess COVID-19 curtailment strategies across Indian states.
Uses EpiEstim-derived time-varying reproduction numbers and correlates them with social-media activity and mobility indicators.
Compares district-level instantaneous reproduction-number methods and concludes that the Jombart process is better suited to guiding local response than alternatives.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit comparative assessment of reproduction-number estimators for operational use.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates Sri Lankan basic and time-varying reproduction numbers using compartmental, exponential-growth, maximum-likelihood, and Bayesian methods.
Analyzes COVID-19 spread in Bushehr using reproduction numbers to test whether rising summer temperature reduced transmission.
Uses Bayesian data augmentation and case-pair information to estimate effective reproduction numbers and assess Singapore's containment measures.
Estimates Italy's net reproduction number after nationwide lockdown and shows that it fell below one.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete monitoring of SARS-CoV-2 transmissibility after intervention.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates bubonic-plague basic reproduction numbers from epidemic doubling time and an SIR model using Zambia outbreak data.
Uses Italy's integrated COVID-19 surveillance data to estimate regional basic and net reproductive numbers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete national surveillance analysis of early SARS-CoV-2 transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares three reproduction-number estimation methods and performs sensitivity analysis of the effect of generation time.
Uses estimated time-varying reproduction numbers to compare heterogeneity and control effectiveness across 25 Chinese cities.
Calculates serotype-specific basic reproduction numbers from laboratory-confirmed HFMD outbreaks reported to China's national surveillance system.
Estimates the reproduction-number range for a COVID-19 outbreak in a Brazilian penitentiary complex.
Estimates COVID-19 reproduction numbers from daily reported cases under multiple generation-time scenarios in Markazi Province.
Monitors reported contacts and associated transmission around distancing and school reopening, but the title and abstract do not explicitly report reproduction-number estimation or surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit reproduction-number method or estimate is described.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Demonstrates bias from incomplete public data and re-estimates COVID-19 basic reproduction numbers using symptom-onset and contact-tracing information.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit bias assessment and improved estimation using more detailed data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates an effective reproduction number from prospectively identified primary and secondary SARS-CoV-2 cases in Barcelona summer schools.
Combines early official incidence growth with a reconstructed generation interval to estimate COVID-19 basic reproduction numbers across Western Europe.
Uses effective reproduction-number estimates with mobility and business-census data to quantify mobility reductions needed to control COVID-19.
Uses EpiEstim to estimate time-varying reproduction-number trajectories across three Canadian provinces.
Uses EpiEstim-derived time-varying reproduction numbers to assess successive phases of Malaysia's movement-control order.
Estimates time-varying reproduction numbers with EpiEstim for Georgia and two subregions and relates changes to restrictions and a superspreading event.
Calculates weekly reproduction numbers from Saudi COVID-19 incidence data to assess whether transmission was controlled.
Estimates a reproduction number for Italy's second COVID-19 wave and uses it to project epidemic size.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 transmission and projection analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses contact-tracing transmission pairs to estimate county-level reproduction-number time courses for SARS-CoV-2 in Georgia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatially resolved COVID-19 transmission analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes effective reproduction-number trajectories before and after relaxation of social-isolation policies in 12 Brazilian cities.
Uses sequential-Bayesian and time-dependent methods to monitor basic and real-time reproduction numbers and forecast COVID-19 incidence in central Iran.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete reproduction-number monitoring and outbreak projection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates effective reproduction numbers from US measles notification data to assess post-elimination transmissibility trends.
Compares five empirical estimators, multiple incidence proxies, and model-based estimates using correlation, agreement, uncertainty, and sensitivity analyses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit benchmarking and robustness assessment of reproduction-number estimation methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits a simple SEIR model with Bayesian inference to estimate country-level COVID-19 basic reproduction numbers across Africa.
Cross-validates random-forest, linear-regression, and regional-averaging approaches for predicting rubella basic reproduction numbers where seroprevalence data are absent.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit comparative performance assessment of estimation approaches.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses South Korean Omicron transmission pairs and maximum likelihood to estimate serial intervals and outbreak reproduction numbers.
Uses generalized growth models to estimate effective reproduction numbers in a prospective Nicaraguan pediatric influenza cohort.
Uses moving-window estimates to quantify global variation in seasonal-influenza effective reproduction numbers from country detection time series.
Presents and validates a bespoke rapid method for calculating current coronavirus growth rates and reproduction numbers from UK case data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development validated against government consensus estimates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates the reproduction number, incubation period, and generation time from the first 255 confirmed Italian monkeypox cases.
Uses EpiEstim and public case data to estimate time-varying reproduction numbers during Fiji's second COVID-19 wave.
Uses a compartmental vector-borne model and reported cases to estimate regional effective reproduction numbers for Brazilian yellow-fever outbreaks.
Introduces a novel nonlinear least-squares curve-fitting method for estimating the basic reproduction number and applies it to the Diamond Princess outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit novel estimation method with outbreak demonstration.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates within-farm African-swine-fever basic reproduction numbers for sows and fattening pigs in two Vietnamese farms.
Fits EpiNow2 to New Zealand case data to estimate time-varying effective reproduction numbers in real time during the Delta outbreak.
Defines an early reproduction-number measure using the new EpiMPX surveillance system and applies it to European mpox cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit definition of a new surveillance-derived reproduction-number measure.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies EpiEstim to daily incidence to estimate spatially refined time-varying reproduction numbers across Georgia health districts.
Estimates Nigeria's time-varying effective mpox reproduction number from case notifications and interprets it for real-time monitoring.
Uses local and national outbreak data with a susceptible-infectious linear-regression approach to estimate between-farm African-swine-fever reproduction numbers.
Estimates basic and time-varying reproduction numbers for five SARS-CoV-2 variants using sequence incidence curves, exponential growth, and EpiEstim.
Estimates effective reproduction numbers from official Spanish mpox surveillance data and compares temporal, regional, and population trends.
Estimates basic and district-level net reproduction numbers for the 2022 Sudan-virus outbreak in Uganda.
Introduces a novel filtering-type Bayesian inference for data-driven instantaneous reproduction-number estimation and applies it to European mpox outbreaks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development with multicountry application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an exponential-growth model and public daily case counts to estimate and compare effective reproduction numbers for mpox in China and other countries.
Calculates weekly-window instantaneous reproduction numbers from mpox surveillance data for six Latin American countries.
Uses exponential-growth, maximum-likelihood, and time-varying approaches to estimate early COVID-19 reproduction numbers across 45 African countries.
Compares reproduction-number trajectories across national and subnational levels and tests whether national monitoring masks localized epidemic trends.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of geographic surveillance resolution using reproduction-number estimates.
